name: Mennonite Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, dissolve the yeast in 1 cup of warm water. Let stand for 5 minutes until the yeast foams.
2. Stir in the salt, oil, honey, remaining water, and buttermilk. Mix in the rye flour, wheat germ, and whole wheat flour; beat well.
3. Add bread flour, 1 cup at a time, beating well until stiff dough forms.
4. Turn the dough out onto lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes, adding additional bread flour as needed.
5. Wash the bowl, grease it, and add the dough to the bowl. Turn over to grease the top of the dough.
6. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled.
7. Grease four 8-inch bread pans.
8. Punch down the dough. Divide into 4 portions.
9. Shape each into a loaf and place in the pans. Cover loosely.
10. Let rise until about doubled.
11. Bake in a preheated oven at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for approximately 40 minutes.
